_comment: By default cherrypick-gex reads the suite's CANONICAL shared stream cache (~/.cherrypick/data/marketdata/stream_cache.db) and writes its own spot-trail history + logs under ~/.cherrypick/data/gex and ~/.cherrypick/logs/gex — omit the path keys to accept those. Piggyback (the common case): some producer streams the canonical cache — MEIC's streamer, or the standalone packages/streamer daemon — and this module reads it read-only; do NOT run this module's streamer. Standalone: only when nothing else is the producer, run `run.py stream` to populate the canonical cache yourself (exactly one streamer writes it at a time). Point source.stream_cache_db elsewhere to override.
_symbols_note: The dashboard's symbol selector and default (symbols[0]) — list the underlyings the source stream cache is actively streaming, most-active first. In piggyback mode these should match the cherrypick-meic streamer's symbols; only symbols the streamer keeps fresh have a live spot/GEX.
